name: openPangu-2.0-Flash (Non-Thinking)
archDisplay: MoE with MLA + DSA/SWA hybrid attention (1:2 ratio), 4-stream mHC residual, multi-token prediction heads; trained on Huawei Ascend NPUs (no NVIDIA hardware)
note: Default (no-extended-reasoning) inference mode of the same openPangu-2.0-Flash weights; this is the mode a user gets without opting into Thinking mode.

results.notes: GPQA-Diamond, Avg@4, Non-Thinking mode column of the official benchmark table
PinchBench, Avg@3, Non-Thinking mode column of the official benchmark table

verificationNotes:
Same official HF model card as the Thinking sibling; Non-Thinking column of the same benchmark table.

2026-07-16: Checked all 5 flagged core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, hle, math, humaneval, mmmu) against (1) the official HF model card README_EN.md — fetched twice, covering all four published tables (General: CL-Bench/IFEval/IFBench/AgentIF/SysBench/Multichallenge; Reasoning: AIME 2026/HMMT/IMO-AnswerBench/BBEH/GPQA-Diamond; Agent: BrowseComp/SkillsBench/PinchBench/Claw-Eval/WildClawBench/MCP-Atlas/TAU2-Bench; Coding: LiveCodeBench V6/DeepCodeBench/SWE-bench Verified/FeatBench) — none of MMLU, HLE, MATH, HumanEval, or MMMU appear anywhere; (2) independent leaderboards — Epoch AI's model database has no openPangu entry, Vals.ai's model registry lists no Huawei models at all, Artificial Analysis's HLE leaderboard does not include openPangu, and LMArena's July 2026 rankings do not surface it; (3) the HF "Add evaluation results" discussion (#4), a bot-generated extraction of the same model card table, independently confirms the same benchmark set with no additions; and (4) the openPangu-2.0-Flash (Thinking) sibling file, sourced from the identical model card, shows the exact same 5 gaps, corroborating this is a genuine absence in the source material rather than a one-file omission. openPangu-2.0-Flash is also text-only (modalities: ["text"], vision: false), so MMMU (a multimodal benchmark) is categorically inapplicable regardless of publication status. Conclusion: all 5 are omitted_no_evidence — Huawei's launch benchmark suite for this release deliberately favors newer/harder evals (AIME 2026, GPQA-Diamond, LiveCodeBench V6, SWE-bench Verified, agentic suites) over legacy saturated ones (MMLU, HumanEval, MATH), and HLE/MMMU were never run or published for this model by any source checked.

2026-07-18 (round-22 hle/mmmu chase): Re-searched specifically for 'hle' and 'mmmu' beyond the 2026-07-16 pass, using live web search/fetch (not just re-reading cached notes). (1) Re-fetched the official HF model card README_EN.md directly and enumerated every table again (General: CL-Bench/IFEval/IFBench/AgentIF/SysBench/Multichallenge; Reasoning: AIME 2026/HMMT Feb 2025/IMO-AnswerBench/BBEH/GPQA-Diamond; Agent: BrowseComp/SkillsBench/PinchBench/Claw-Eval/WildClawBench/MCP-Atlas/TAU2-Bench; Coding: LiveCodeBench V6/DeepCodeBench/SWE-bench Verified/FeatBench) — HLE and MMMU absent from all four. (2) Re-fetched HF discussion #4 'Add evaluation results' (bot-generated eval-results PR against this exact model card) — confirmed it lists only AIME 2026, GPQA-Diamond, Claw-Eval, WildClawBench, SWE-bench Verified as machine-readable additions, explicitly skips 15 other named benchmarks (none of which is HLE or MMMU) as 'not on hub'. (3) Checked independent trackers by name: Epoch AI benchmarking hub, BenchLM.ai (316 benchmarks indexed), Scale Labs leaderboard, Vals.ai, llm-stats.com, Artificial Analysis HLE leaderboard — none returned an openPangu/Huawei entry of any kind. (4) Searched for sibling/comparison citations in third-party launch roundups (Chinese open-weight model comparison articles covering Pangu vs Qwen/DeepSeek/Kimi/GLM/MiMo, June-July 2026) — these discuss Pangu's cost/sovereignty positioning but cite no HLE or MMMU number for it. (5) Searched arXiv for openPangu used as an eval baseline; the one nominal hit (a WebSearch-synthesized claim of 'OpenPangu-72B' HLE candidate-identification percentages, arXiv 2602.04496 'ReThinker') did NOT survive verification — directly fetching and querying the paper's actual PDF text confirmed it does not mention OpenPangu-72B, openPangu-2.0-Flash, or any such HLE figures at all; the search-engine summary appears to be a synthesis artifact, not a real citation, and '72B' does not match this model's published 92B-total/6B-active configuration in any case, so it was discarded as non-credible rather than proposed. HLE conclusion: omitted_no_evidence, now checked against 5 of 6 required methods (model card, independent trackers, academic/arXiv, GitHub-adjacent HF discussion, sibling comparisons) with no credible number surfacing anywhere — this appears to be a genuine, not-yet-published gap for this specific model rather than a research shortfall. MMMU conclusion: confirmed architecturally inapplicable — corpus modalities field is ["text"] and capabilities.vision is false for this text-only model, so MMMU (a vision-reasoning benchmark) cannot apply regardless of publication status; no vision variant of openPangu-2.0-Flash exists per the same model card.
